sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the left green square is now inside the left frame! The frame has disappeared (just like the right frame did when the right green square was placed). Both frames are now gone, and both green squares are in their respective positions. The level should be complete now. Let me check if the level is done.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
greens = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N']
print("Green squares:")
for n in greens:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("\nCenters:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} at {n['boundary']}")
print("\nFrames (c, pixels>=15):")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] >= 15:
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}")
print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The left frame is still visible at [32,11]-[36,15], and the left green square is at [33,12]-[35,14]. The green square is inside the frame but the frame hasn't disappeared yet. This is different from what happened with the right frame.
Wait, looking more carefully:
- Right green: [27,42]-[29,44], center at [28,43] with color c
- Left green: [33,12]-[35,14], center at [34,13] with color W
The right frame disappeared when the right green was placed, but the left frame is still there. Maybe the level isn't complete because the green squares need to have specific center colors? The right green has c center and the left green has W center.
Or maybe I need to look at this differently. The right frame disappeared when the green square was placed at [27,42]-[29,44], which was inside the frame at [26,44]-[30,48]. But the left frame at [32,11]-[36,15] still exists even though the green is at [33,12]-[35,14] inside it.
Hmm, let me check if maybe the right frame actually didn't disappear - maybe I miscounted. Let me look at the frames again. The output shows only one frame at [32,11]-[36,15] and the bottom bar at [63,0]-[63,47]. So the right frame did disappear.
But the left frame is still there. Maybe the level requires both green squares to have the same center color? Currently left=W and right=c. Let me try clicking the left green to swap the colors.
Actually wait - maybe the left green square needs to be in a different position. The frame is at [32,11]-[36,15], which is a 5x5 area. The interior is [33,12]-[35,14], which is 3x3. The green square is at [33,12]-[35,14], which is exactly the interior. So the position should be correct.
Let me try clicking the left green square to see if that triggers the frame to disappear.

# Frame still visible - maybe need to click to "lock" it in place
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 34, 'col': 13}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
